config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the individual, because that can easily become an excuse to ignore basic rights and silence minority voices. In my view, a healthy society is built by protecting individual dignity, freedom, and fairness first, then balancing those with the common good. From my perspective, the state and the majority should not be trusted to decide whose rights are convenient to sacrifice.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kita justru penuh dengan contoh ketika “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan pembungkaman minoritas, diskriminasi, dan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi hanya sah jika dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tidak boleh dikorbankan seenaknya. Kalau hak individu tidak diprioritaskan, masyarakat bisa berubah menjadi mayoritarianisme yang keras dan tidak adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from Indonesia, that kind of idea can too easily be used to justify pressure from the majority, social conformity, and even abuse by authorities in the name of order or harmony. Society matters, but it should be built on protecting individual rights first, because once basic rights are weakened, the so-called common good often ends up serving the powerful more than the public.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat betapa sering alasan “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan diskriminasi, sensor, dan perluasan kuasa negara, jadi saya sangat curiga pada klaim mayoritas yang ingin mengorbankan hak dasar. Bagi saya, masyarakat memang penting, tetapi justru masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang membatasi diri lewat hak individu yang kuat, bukan yang menekan individu atas nama ketertiban. Kalau hak dasar bisa dikalahkan kapan saja oleh suara terbanyak, itu bukan keadilan, melainkan dominasi mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take automatic priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from Indonesia, appeals to “harmony,” “order,” or “the common good” are often used to pressure people into silence, especially minorities or anyone seen as inconvenient to the majority. A society is only truly healthy when it protects individual rights first and then seeks the common good without trampling basic dignity. If society can override rights whenever it wants, then the phrase “for the public interest” becomes a very easy cover for power abuse.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh diposisikan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, justru pengalaman politik kita menunjukkan bahwa alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan sensor, diskriminasi, dan perluasan kekuasaan yang berbahaya. Saya juga tidak setuju dengan gagasan bahwa harmoni atau ketertiban otomatis lebih bernilai daripada kebebasan dasar; masyarakat yang sehat harus membatasi diri lewat hak yang kuat, bukan menekan orang yang tidak populer. Kepentingan publik penting, tetapi hanya sah kalau tidak mengorbankan martabat dan kebebasan individu seenaknya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
